analyses.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
analyses.verdict: Cleanest technical setup in today's scan, backed by real earnings acceleration -- buy the breakout, trim before the July 15 print.

financials.summary: The world's largest custody bank continued to benefit from higher markets (fee income scales with assets under custody/administration) and higher-for-longer short rates on its deposit book. Net interest income and fee income both grew, expenses stayed disciplined, and the bank kept returning capital via buybacks.
highlights: EPS growth of roughly +42% YoY per the latest trailing figures
Revenue growth of roughly +13% YoY
Continued share buybacks supporting EPS growth on top of operating gains
Assets under custody/administration near record levels on higher equity markets

accomplishments: Continued expansion of digital-assets/tokenization custody services for institutional clients
Ongoing buyback program shrinking share count
Record or near-record levels of assets under custody

journey_52w.summary: BK spent the past year in a steady Stage 2 uptrend, grinding higher with only shallow pullbacks -- the kind of low-volatility grind institutions favor. It's now coiling in its tightest base of the year (3% contraction) just 2.5% under its 52-week high.

milestones.label: Q3 2025
milestones.note: Beat on EPS driven by fee-income strength

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Guided higher on net interest income amid the rate environment

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Tightened into a 3% VCP just under its 52-week high

risks: Earnings on July 15 is a binary event after a big run
Custody fee income is sensitive to an equity-market drawdown
catalysts: Continued AUC/AUA growth on record equity markets
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~14x is reasonable for a business growing EPS over 40%

analyses.name: JPMorgan Chase
analyses.verdict: Best-in-breed money-center bank riding a clean breakout into earnings -- TradingView's strongest financial-sector read of the day backs the call.

financials.summary: Record profitability across investment banking, trading, and consumer segments; net interest income stayed resilient even as rates plateaued, and investment-banking fees recovered alongside stronger capital-markets activity.
highlights: EPS +17% YoY
Revenue +13% YoY
Continued buybacks and a growing dividend
Investment-banking fees recovering with capital-markets activity

accomplishments: Continued gains in trading and investment-banking market share
Strong capital ratios supporting buybacks
Ongoing technology/AI investment driving efficiency gains

journey_52w.summary: A steady grind higher over the past year with occasional pullbacks on rate-path uncertainty, resuming its uptrend as capital-markets activity picked up into 2026.

milestones.label: Q4 2025
milestones.note: Record annual profit reported

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Trading and investment-banking fees beat estimates

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Tightened into a 2.3% VCP near all-time highs

risks: Earnings in ~12 days -- binary risk
RSI 68 with little room left before the 52-week high
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~14x is cheap for the highest-quality large-cap bank franchise

analyses.name: Eli Lilly
analyses.verdict: The clearest fundamental story in the whole scan -- the GLP-1 franchise is driving triple-digit EPS growth -- and TradingView agrees.

financials.summary: Zepbound and Mounjaro sales continued scaling rapidly, driving explosive revenue and EPS growth, alongside heavy ongoing capex investment into manufacturing capacity to relieve supply constraints.
highlights: EPS +170% YoY
Revenue +55% YoY -- the GLP-1 franchise is the primary growth driver
Continued heavy capex into manufacturing capacity
Consistent beat-and-raise quarters

accomplishments: Continued GLP-1 manufacturing capacity expansion to meet demand
Pipeline progress in oral GLP-1 and metabolic/Alzheimer's programs
Multiple consecutive beat-and-raise quarters

journey_52w.summary: A strong uptrend over the past year with volatility around competitive GLP-1 headlines (Novo Nordisk, emerging oral candidates) and drug-pricing/political scrutiny, but fundamentals kept overwhelming the noise.

milestones.label: Q4 2025
milestones.note: GLP-1 sales beat, FY guidance raised

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Continued triple-digit obesity-drug revenue growth

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Basing just 1.5% under its 52-week high

risks: US drug-pricing/political headline risk
Competitive GLP-1 landscape (Novo Nordisk, emerging oral candidates)
High-dollar stock ($1,200+) means larger point-swings
catalysts: Continued capacity ramp easing supply constraints
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~27x is full but justified by the growth rate -- growth, not multiple expansion, is doing the work here

analyses.name: Apple
analyses.verdict: Steady mega-cap climb with the broadest technical confirmation of any pick today -- services growth keeps cushioning hardware cyclicality.

financials.summary: Services revenue continued double-digit growth, iPhone demand held steady, gross margins stayed near record highs, and the buyback program continued at scale.
highlights: EPS +22% YoY
Revenue +17% YoY
Services-segment margin near record levels
Continued large-scale buyback program shrinking share count

accomplishments: Apple Intelligence rollout continuing across the device ecosystem
Record services revenue (App Store, subscriptions, advertising)
Continued expansion in wearables and emerging markets

journey_52w.summary: After a choppy stretch tied to AI-strategy skepticism and China-demand questions, the stock resumed its uptrend as services growth and buybacks reasserted themselves -- a wide, patient base rather than an explosive breakout.

milestones.label: Q4 FY2025
milestones.note: Record services revenue reported

milestones.label: Q1 CY2026
milestones.note: iPhone demand steadied, AI-feature rollout continued

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Basing in an 8%-wide range just 2% under its 52-week high

risks: China smartphone demand/competition
Regulatory pressure on App Store economics
Earnings in ~29 days
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~32x -- a premium multiple, backed by best-in-class capital returns and services growth

analyses.name: Fortinet
analyses.verdict: Durable double-digit cybersecurity grower with a tight base -- a steady compounder, not a moonshot.

financials.summary: Continued strong billings growth as the SASE/cloud-security platform gained share against legacy network-security competitors, with healthy free-cash-flow margins.
highlights: EPS +29% YoY
Revenue +20% YoY
Continued platform consolidation (SASE, cloud security) driving cross-sell
Healthy free-cash-flow margins

accomplishments: Continued expansion of the SASE/cloud-security platform
Share gains against legacy network-security competitors
Consistent double-digit billings growth

journey_52w.summary: A steady grind higher over the past year as enterprise security budgets held up better than broader IT spend, with only shallow pullbacks.

milestones.label: Q4 2025
milestones.note: Billings growth beat, double-digit revenue guide reaffirmed

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Continued SASE platform momentum

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Tightened into a 3.6% VCP near highs

risks: Richest valuation in the tech group (fwd P/E ~46x)
Earnings in ~28 days
Competitive cybersecurity landscape (Palo Alto, CrowdStrike, Zscaler)
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~46x is the richest multiple among the tech picks -- priced for continued 20%+ growth

analyses.name: Applied Materials
analyses.verdict: Quietly repairing while megacap-tech peers stay broken -- the tightest base in the whole scan, still 17% under its high.

financials.summary: Semiconductor-equipment demand recovering unevenly by segment -- leading-edge logic/foundry capex strong on AI tailwinds, while NAND/DRAM equipment demand lagged.
highlights: EPS +34% YoY
Revenue +11% YoY
AI-driven leading-edge logic/foundry capex a key growth driver
Memory (NAND/DRAM) equipment demand lagging the logic recovery

accomplishments: Continued share in leading-edge (sub-3nm) equipment tools
Growing services/AGS (Applied Global Services) recurring-revenue base
AI-datacenter-driven fab capex supporting bookings

journey_52w.summary: A choppy year, hit alongside the broader semiconductor-equipment complex on export-control and cyclical-inventory concerns, but repairing over the past month with the fastest relative-strength move in this entire scan.

milestones.label: Q4 2025
milestones.note: China export-control headwinds weighed on the stock

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Bookings stabilized on AI/logic capex strength

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Sharpest 4-week RS turn (+22%) in the scan while NVDA/INTC/MU stayed weak

risks: US-China export-control policy remains the single biggest swing factor
Still 17% below its 52-week high -- an early repair, not a confirmed new uptrend
Earnings 43 days out
catalysts: Continued AI/logic capex commentary from TSMC/Intel/Samsung
watch: NAND/DRAM equipment order trends for a broader semi-cycle turn
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~37x, reasonable given the growth rate if the repair holds

analyses.name: American Electric Power
analyses.verdict: Direct AI-data-center power-demand beneficiary -- TradingView's cleanest utility-sector read of the day, zero sell votes.

financials.summary: Regulated utility with a growing rate base, increasingly driven by data-center interconnection demand across its multi-state footprint, alongside constructive regulatory outcomes.
highlights: EPS +7% YoY
Revenue +10% YoY
Data-center/large-load interconnection requests driving multi-year capex and rate-base growth guidance higher
Constructive regulatory outcomes across several jurisdictions

accomplishments: Raised long-term capex/rate-base growth guidance on data-center demand
Continued grid-modernization investment
Constructive rate-case settlements

journey_52w.summary: Utilities broadly re-rated over the past year as AI data-center power demand became a mainstream investment theme; AEP has been a prime beneficiary given its exposure to data-center-heavy states.

milestones.label: Q3 2025
milestones.note: Raised long-term load-growth guidance on data-center demand

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Continued data-center interconnection request growth

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: RSI warmed to 73 as the AI-power-demand rotation accelerated

risks: RSI 73 is warm -- limited room before overbought
Regulatory/rate-case outcomes are never guaranteed
Typical utility-sector interest-rate sensitivity
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~20x -- a full multiple for a regulated utility, reflecting the AI-power-demand re-rating

analyses.name: Starbucks
analyses.verdict: Turnaround initiatives are showing up in the numbers -- TradingView's zero-sell-vote STRONG_BUY backs the technical read.

financials.summary: The 'Back to Starbucks' turnaround plan showed early traction on US comparable sales and cost discipline, while China remains a mixed, competitive market.
highlights: EPS +33% YoY
Revenue +9% YoY
US comparable-sales trends improving under the turnaround plan
Cost/efficiency initiatives supporting margin recovery

accomplishments: Continued rollout of the 'Back to Starbucks' turnaround initiatives
Store-level efficiency and menu-simplification progress
Stabilizing US traffic trends

journey_52w.summary: The stock spent much of the past year range-bound to lower amid turnaround skepticism and China competitive pressure (Luckin Coffee and local players), before stabilizing and turning higher as comps improved.

milestones.label: Q4 FY2025
milestones.note: US comps showed early turnaround traction

milestones.label: Q1 FY2026
milestones.note: Margin recovery continued

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Confirmed uptrend with EPS +33% YoY behind it

risks: Fwd P/E ~35x prices in continued turnaround success
China market remains competitive (Luckin and local players)
Revenue growth (+8.8%) more modest than EPS growth -- margin-driven, not demand-driven
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~35x -- a premium that assumes the turnaround keeps delivering

analyses.name: Moderna
analyses.verdict: TradingView loves it and the score is the highest in the scan, but RSI 86 and a +57% 4-week RS surge make this the most extended name on the list -- watch, don't chase.

financials.summary: Revenue swung sharply on COVID-vaccine order-timing comparisons; the RSV vaccine (mResvia) and pipeline programs continued scaling as the company worked toward a lower, sustainable post-pandemic break-even revenue level.
highlights: Revenue +260% YoY -- almost certainly an easy comparison against a deeply depressed prior-year base, not a durable growth rate
Continued cost-cutting to reach a lower break-even revenue level
RSV vaccine and combination-shot pipeline progressing

accomplishments: Continued diversification beyond COVID vaccines into RSV and combination respiratory shots
Pipeline progress in individualized cancer vaccines (with partners)
Aggressive cost restructuring to right-size the post-pandemic cost base

journey_52w.summary: The stock spent most of the past year in a deep, multi-year downtrend as COVID-vaccine revenue collapsed, before a sharp, parabolic reversal over the last month that has pushed RSI to the most extended level in the entire scan.

milestones.label: 2H 2025
milestones.note: Continued COVID-revenue decline, restructuring announcements

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: RSV vaccine and pipeline updates

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Sharp, parabolic +57% 4-week RS surge -- the most extended setup in the scan

risks: RSI 86 -- highest in the scan, high odds of a sharp mean-reversion
Revenue base remains structurally lower than the pandemic peak
Binary pipeline/regulatory catalysts create event risk
watch: Whether the rally consolidates (healthy) or reverses sharply (typical after this kind of RSI extreme)
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E is negative (still unprofitable on a trailing basis) -- this is a story/turnaround stock, not a value one

analyses.name: GE Aerospace
analyses.verdict: Most overbought name in the entire scan (RSI 91) riding an aerospace-cycle narrative -- earnings growth is actually negative, so the rally is running on revenue and sentiment, not the bottom line.

financials.summary: GE Aerospace benefited from strong commercial-engine services and spare-parts demand as global air travel and aircraft utilization stayed elevated; EPS was pressured by one-off items and mix effects despite solid revenue growth.
highlights: Revenue +25% YoY -- strong commercial aerospace services demand
EPS -2% YoY despite the revenue growth -- one-off charges/mix effects offsetting operating strength
Services/spare-parts (higher-margin, recurring) mix continuing to grow as a share of revenue

accomplishments: Continued growth in engine services and spare-parts revenue as global fleets fly more hours
Backlog at multi-year highs on commercial engine orders
Progress on LEAP engine production ramp supporting Boeing/Airbus narrow-body output

journey_52w.summary: The stock has been in a strong, largely uninterrupted uptrend over the past year as the aerospace-recovery and services-mix-shift story gained investor conviction, pushing RSI to an extreme 91 -- the most overbought reading in this scan.

milestones.label: Q4 2025
milestones.note: Backlog and services revenue hit new highs

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: LEAP engine production ramp continued

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: RSI reached 91 -- the most extended reading in the entire scan

risks: RSI 91 -- most overbought name in the scan, high odds of a sharp pullback or extended consolidation
EPS actually declined YoY despite the stock's strength -- a disconnect worth watching into the print
Earnings in 14 days into an extremely stretched setup
catalysts: Continued LEAP engine ramp/Boeing-Airbus delivery-rate commentary
watch: Whether EPS reconciles with the revenue growth in the next print, or whether the gap widens
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~44x is one of the richest multiples in this whole scan, on declining EPS -- a name priced for a services-mix re-rating story, not near-term earnings

analyses.name: Procter & Gamble
analyses.verdict: Not one of today's Top Picks -- the scan flags a broken trend (still below its 150/200-day) despite an ongoing base -- a name to watch for repair, not to buy on the current bounce.

financials.summary: Steady, low-single-digit organic sales growth, with price/mix offsetting soft volumes in some categories; continued strong free cash flow and a multi-decade dividend-growth streak.
highlights: EPS +6% YoY
Revenue +7% YoY
Organic sales growth modest -- price/mix carrying more of the load than volume
Continued strong free cash flow supporting the dividend

accomplishments: Continued multi-decade dividend-growth streak
Ongoing productivity/cost-savings program
Steady market-share performance in most core categories

journey_52w.summary: P&G has underperformed the broader market over the past year, down as much as 20% from its highs on soft volume trends and consumer trade-down pressure in some categories, only recently stabilizing into a multi-contraction base.

milestones.label: 2H 2025
milestones.note: Soft volume trends in several categories weighed on the stock

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Price/mix continued to offset volume softness

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: 8.8% off its high, basing (6-contraction VCP, 3.6% tight) but still below its 150/200-day lines

risks: Trend still broken -- below both the 150- and 200-day moving averages despite the tightening base
Volume growth remains soft; consumer trade-down risk in a slower economy
No TradingView cross-check resolved for this ticker
watch: Volume trends vs. price/mix in the next print
A reclaim of the 150/200-day would be the first real repair signal
critical.valuation: Fwd P/E ~21x -- full for the current low-single-digit growth profile; needs volume reacceleration to justify chasing

analyses.name: Coinbase Global
analyses.verdict: TradingView reads NEUTRAL -- a coin-flip technical picture. Not in today's scanned universe; fundamentally levered to crypto-market activity and regulatory clarity, both wildcards right now.

financials.summary: Trading volumes and revenue remained highly correlated with crypto-market activity (BTC/ETH price action), while subscription/services revenue (custody, staking, USDC yield) kept growing as a diversification effort away from pure transaction revenue.
highlights: Revenue remains heavily levered to trading volume, which swings sharply with crypto prices
Subscription & services revenue (custody, staking, stablecoin yield) a growing, more durable mix shift
US crypto regulatory environment has been gradually clarifying, a tailwind for the business model

accomplishments: Continued build-out of subscription/services revenue to reduce trading-volume dependence
Expanded institutional custody and staking offerings
Benefiting from an incrementally clearer US regulatory stance on crypto

journey_52w.summary: Coinbase's stock has tracked crypto-market sentiment closely over the past year -- rallying hard during bullish BTC/ETH stretches and giving back gains during risk-off crypto drawdowns. TradingView's current NEUTRAL read reflects that two-sided setup.

milestones.label: 2H 2025
milestones.note: Rallied alongside a strong crypto-market stretch

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Gave back some gains during a crypto-market pullback

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: TradingView rates the setup NEUTRAL (9 buy / 7 sell / 10 neutral)

risks: Revenue remains highly correlated to and dependent on crypto-market trading volumes and prices
Regulatory risk, while improving, is not fully resolved
Not in today's scanned universe -- no live quant/VCP read available
catalysts: Further US regulatory clarity (stablecoin, market-structure legislation)
watch: Trading-volume trends in the next print
Subscription/services revenue as a % of total (the diversification thesis)
critical.valuation: Trades on crypto-market sentiment as much as on fundamentals -- treat as a high-beta crypto proxy

analyses.name: Sandisk
analyses.verdict: TradingView rates this an outright SELL (6 buy / 11 sell) -- the NAND-flash memory cycle is historically volatile and the technical picture is currently negative.

financials.summary: Standalone NAND-flash memory maker (spun off from Western Digital) navigating a cyclical, historically volatile memory-pricing environment, with margins highly sensitive to NAND pricing swings.
highlights: NAND flash pricing is the dominant swing factor for revenue and margins
Standalone balance sheet post-spinoff carries the debt load allocated in the separation
Memory-market cycles have historically been sharp in both directions

accomplishments: Completed spinoff from Western Digital as an independent, pure-play NAND flash company
Continued technology-roadmap investment (next-gen NAND nodes) alongside joint-venture partner Kioxia

journey_52w.summary: Since separating from Western Digital, SNDK has traded with high volatility tied to NAND pricing headlines and the broader memory cycle; TradingView's current SELL rating reflects a technical picture that has turned negative.

milestones.label: Post-spinoff 2025
milestones.note: Began trading as an independent NAND-flash pure play

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: NAND pricing volatility weighed on margins

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: TradingView rates the setup SELL (6 buy / 11 sell / 9 neutral)

risks: TradingView SELL rating -- the technical picture is currently negative
NAND pricing is highly cyclical and difficult to forecast
Standalone post-spinoff balance sheet carries real leverage
catalysts: A NAND pricing trend inflection (has been known to turn sharply)
Data-center/AI-storage demand growth as a structural tailwind
critical.valuation: Best assessed as a cyclical memory play -- valuation swings with the NAND cycle more than with steady per-share earnings

analyses.name: Butterfly Network
analyses.verdict: No TradingView rating resolved and not in today's scanned universe -- a small-cap medical-device story stock; size any position accordingly.

financials.summary: Point-of-care ultrasound device maker (the handheld 'ultrasound-on-a-chip') still working toward sustained profitability, with revenue growth driven by device placements and subscription/software attach.
highlights: Revenue growth driven by continued handheld-ultrasound device placements
Software/subscription attach rate is the key long-term margin driver
Company has historically been unprofitable while scaling the platform

accomplishments: Continued expansion of the handheld point-of-care ultrasound installed base
Ongoing software/AI-guidance feature development to increase subscription attach
Expanding use cases beyond radiology into emergency medicine and primary care

journey_52w.summary: BFLY remains a small-cap, high-volatility story stock -- its share price has swung on financing news, device-adoption updates, and broader small-cap risk appetite rather than steady fundamentals.

milestones.note: Continued device-placement growth and software-feature rollout

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: No TradingView technical rating currently resolved for this ticker

risks: Not profitable -- still burning cash to fund growth
No independent technical (TV) read available
Small-cap liquidity and volatility risk
critical.valuation: Speculative, pre-profitability small-cap -- valued on adoption trajectory, not current earnings
